revision.text:
[[ja:朝代神社]] '''Asashiro Shrine''' (朝代神社) {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Asashiro Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Asashiro-jinja06s3200.jpg | location = {{ill|Maizuru|en|Maizuru|qid=Q750573|lt=Maizuru|1=Maizuru}}, Kyoto Prefecture, Asashiro 13 | iso = JP-26 | latitude = 35|longitude = 135|latd = 35|latm = 26|lats = 38.6|longd = 135|longm = 19|longs = 26.4 | deity = {{ill|Izanagi|en|Izanagi|qid=Q813858|lt=Izanagi|1=Izanagi}} | object = | Rank = Prefectural Shrine | established = Traditionally 672 AD | main_building = | other_names = | pilgrimage = | festivals = May 3 (Spring Grand Festival)
November 3 (Autumn Grand Festival) | rites = "Yoshihara Tachi-furi" (once every 4 years) | map = Japan Kyoto | location = {{ill|Maizuru|qid=Q750573|1=Maizuru}}| coordinates = {{Coord|35.44405555555556|N|135.324|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}} '''Asashiro Shrine''' (あさしろじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Maizuru|en|Maizuru|qid=Q750573|lt=Maizuru|1=Maizuru}}, Kyoto Prefecture. The shrine was historically classified as a Prefectural Shrine under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qid=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. == Deities == ; Main deity * {{ill|Izanagi|en|Izanagi|qid=Q813858|lt=Izanagi|1=Izanagi}} (Izanagi-no-Mikoto) == History == The shrine is traditionally said to have been established in September 672, with its deity enshrined from the {{ill|Izanagi Shrine|en|Izanagi Shrine|qid=Q10884977|lt=Izanagi Shrine|1=Izanagi Shrine}} on {{ill|Awaji Island|en|Awaji Island|qid=Q504373|lt=Awaji Island|1=Awaji Island}}. Since the Edo period, it has served as the guardian deity ({{ill|Ubusunagami|en|Ubusunagami|qid=Q1392097|lt=Ubusunagami|1=Ubusunagami}}) of the townsfolk of {{ill|Tango-Tanabe Domain|en|Tango-Tanabe Domain|qid=Q10863423|lt=Tango-Tanabe Domain|1=Tango-Tanabe Domain}}. A record compiled in the late Edo period, "Shingi Kanrei Yoshida-ke Shokoku Shaka Shisso-ki," lists it as one of the eleven notable shrines in {{ill|Tango Province|en|Tango Province|qid=Q1054331|lt=Tango Province|1=Tango Province}}. In 1732, a major fire destroyed the original shrine buildings, which were rebuilt in 1739. == Grounds == After passing through the torii gate, a path leads to the shrine grounds, which are connected to the precincts of {{ill|Enryu-ji|lt=Enryu-ji|lt=Enryu-ji (Maizuru)|ja|円隆寺 (舞鶴市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enryu-ji (Maizuru)|qq=}}. A stone stairway leads to the main hall at the end of the path. File:Maizuru Asashiro-jinja 2019-05 ac (1).jpg|Torii gate File:Asashiro-jinja00s3200.jpg|Pathway File:Maizuru Asashiro-jinja 2019-05 ac (4).jpg|Main hall == Cultural Properties == === Kyoto Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * '''"Yoshihara Tachi-furi"''' – A ritual performed once every four years on November 3, believed to reenact a battle during the {{ill|Siege of Tanabe Castle|ja|田辺城攻防戦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siege of Tanabe Castle|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} during the {{ill|Battle of Sekigahara|en|Battle of Sekigahara|qid=Q234188|lt=Battle of Sekigahara|1=Battle of Sekigahara}}. It is also called "Higashi Yoshihara no Furimono" and has been preserved by the Yoshihara Tachi-furi Preservation Society. Designated as a Maizuru City Intangible Folk Cultural Property in 1968 and as a Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Property in 1989. === Maizuru City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * '''"Asashiro Shrine Main Hall" (Structure)''' – Designated on January 12, 1993[https://www.city.maizuru.kyoto.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000002/2476/maidurusibunkazaiitiran.pdf National and Prefectural Cultural Properties] (Maizuru City). === Maizuru City Designated Tangible Folk Cultural Properties === * '''"Asashiro Shrine Offering Boat"''' – Constructed in 1739. Designated on January 12, 1993. * '''"Asashiro Shrine Festival Emaki"''' – Designated on August 25, 2005 (privately owned). * '''"Asashiro Shrine Festival Floats and Curtain Banners"''' (7 units) – Designated between 2013 and 2015[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/274746 Asashiro Shrine Festival Floats and Curtain Banners] Cultural Heritage Online. == Basic Information == * Address: 13 Asashiro, Maizuru City, Kyoto Prefecture, 624-0842 * Access: 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Nishi-Maizuru Station|en|Nishi-Maizuru Station|qid=Q8192400|lt=Nishi-Maizuru Station|1=Nishi-Maizuru Station}} on the JR West {{ill|Maizuru Line|en|Maizuru Line|qid=Q898812|lt=Maizuru Line|1=Maizuru Line}} or Kyoto Tango Railway Miyamai Line == Nearby locations == * {{ill|Enryu-ji|lt=Enryu-ji|lt=Enryu-ji (Maizuru)|ja|円隆寺 (舞鶴市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enryu-ji (Maizuru)|qq=}} * {{ill|Hongyo-ji|ja|本行寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hongyo-ji|1=Hongyo-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11520793}} * {{ill|Keirin-ji (Maizuru)|ja|桂林寺 (舞鶴市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keirin-ji (Maizuru)|1=Keirin-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11536791}} * {{ill|Zuiko-ji (Maizuru)|ja|瑞光寺 (舞鶴市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zuiko-ji (Maizuru)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == References == {{Reflist|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat-inline|Asashiro-jinja|qq=}} {{shinto-stub|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|朝代神社|version=100338354|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:朝代神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11517252}} [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Kyoto]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Maizuru, Kyoto]] [[Category:舞鶴市の歴史]]
